_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of products and nomenclature of major vendors (e.g., security suites - Trend Micro, Symantec, McAfee, Outpost, Panda, Kaspersky) and how differences affect exploitation/vulnerabilities.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of the data and AI vendor ecosystem, including major platform providers, emerging technology categories, vendor lock-in risks, open-source alternatives, and strategic partnership evaluation criteria.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-K-011 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-014 (DA, cosine: 0.473), SP-K-013 (SP, cosine: 0.452).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.8/10 (raw 0.480). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, major]. DCWF-only terms [affect, differences, exploitation, kaspersky, mcafee, micro] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, alternatives, categories, criteria, data, ecosystem] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.848 (P=0.824, R=0.874). R exceeds P by 0.050 — the WIDAI KSA's 25 words are well-captured by the DCWF element's 24 words, but the DCWF element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (98.6%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of products and nomenclature of major vendors (e.g., security suites -" and "Knowledge of the data and AI vendor ecosystem, including major platform provider". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.535 > STS 0.480 (gap +0.055). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge, major] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.1%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, major]. DCWF-only: [affect, differences, exploitation, kaspersky, mcafee, micro]. WIDAI-only: [ai, alternatives, categories, criteria, data, ecosystem].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 98.6% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
